lines.text: Me? I'm a traveling Taoist from <te href=850154>Mengzhou</te>. Just call me Buling.
Exorcism, talisman divination, fortune-telling... you name it, I "bling" it! So {PlayerName}, you want to try your luck fah?
Heard about you from an old friend. You know Jianxin? Pretty sure you met her in Jinzhou. She trained with the same master before me.
We've got history. Last time I went up to the mountain to see her, she mentioned you.
So I hear you're looking for someone? Tsk, not so easy, not so easy. The feng shui in Rinascita is not good.
The Qi is trapped underwater, not good flow. No luck now, bad sign. You should be careful, Boss!
You show up and the stars change... I was betting on who could break the deadlock, so I'm glad I caught you first.
Boss, why the long face huh? Something getting you down?
I'm here for sightseeing! How could I miss the Great Agon? It's Septimont's signature event!
Ah, you are the one to break the deadlock.
You've noticed it, haven't you, Boss? Septimont is caught in some <color=RedD>anomaly</color>.
I can "feel" it, but I don't know where it's coming from. Every time I get close, it slips away like smoke.
But now, I'm sure of it. If I want out of this mess, I need to borrow some of your muscle.
Oyo, but Taoists have their rules, can't say too much. But helping you helps me... A little nudge is no big deal, right?
Here, take this. A little something from me to you. A good start for us. Anyway, it's on me. No charge.
Seeing isn't believing, but truth exists in the mirror of the world. Go ahead, see for yourself.
Boss! What a coincidence! You here for the cable car too?
From up here, the Septimont view is so good! Before I go, I think I will ride a few more times, definitely.
Yes! My holiday fun in Septimont is already finished. Time to go back.
You keep it, no need to return! It's a little token of good fortune for us.
What's real or illusion, hard to say now. Master always said I had bad luck. Easy for me to get into trouble. Guess he was right!
I had no idea! For a Taoist like me, it felt like... I dunno, some kind of "out-of-body" thing.
I asked for your help because... it just felt right. Master always said no need to overthink these things.
Bye bye, Boss! You're a very fun person.
Who knows, maybe we'll meet again someday?
Alright, I think I got the whole picture now... Oh {PlayerName}, how do you keep ending up in messes like this? This is one tough cookie, all right.
Let me tell you, the latest data we got from the Resonance Nexus isn't so pretty. Something's really off with the Dark Tide in that Sanguis place. I don't remember it being this crazy before.
Normally, the Dark Tide getting all freaky during High Tide is whatever, right? But now, I don't know what happened, cuz it's... it's turning into a person.
So listen up, I'll give you Tethys's rundown. That humanoid thing, that "Sovereign," is gobbling up frequencies like a maniac!
It's eating anything with energy. Dark Tide stuff, Tacet Discords, Echoes, you name it. Might even be saving other things for dessert.
Basically, the Sovereign isn't gonna attack Septimont or Ragunna anytime soon. It's too busy getting fat first. Once it's stuffed full of energy, it'll try and smash the Earthrend Wedge in one go.

With the Earthrender gone, POOF—all that Dark Tide under the hunting grounds will explode like a pimple. When that happens, it's game over for Rinascita. Even if you have three heads and six arms, it will be bye-bye.
But smooth moves, team. Cutting through the clutter, I see what you're doing.
This Corrosaurus is just the bait to hook the big fish, isn't it? Your real plan is to stop the Sovereign before it fully forms, nip it in the bud.
Am I right, Boss?
Heaven and earth are in harmony... That's my reading, so your plan shouldn't have fallen apart like that.
But then again... smooth sailing isn't always a good thing either. Too much harmony invites trouble. Tethys did pick up a strange frequency midway through the hunt.
Everything was fine until that frequency came out of nowhere, and suddenly the Sovereign took form way ahead of schedule. Talk about flipping the table.
Umm. Well. Let me get another reading... The Chi's all messed up, it's kind of hard to see.
It's like someone clouded the heavenly signs on purpose...
That's right. Supernatural powers or not, the Sovereign can't escape good old Tethys. Lemme see... Yep, it's wandering in an area with a whole lot of other frequencies...
Looks like it's east of the Sanguis Plateaus. Yeah, that place has lots of dark energy. Bet many tough people died there.
Boss, what's going on?! I got a bad feeling just now, and Tethys won't stop pumping out alerts.
Aha… figures. That area you're in has buried many warriors. For the Sovereign, it's like having a free buffet.
And sure enough, you blink and it's already swallowed up all the surrounding frequencies. Now it's a real nightmare.
That said…

The situation's bad, no doubt. But we're not out of moves yet... The Dark Tide isn't spreading at full speed. If we act fast and find the Sovereign's weak spot, we might just pull a miracle for Rinascita.

Let's see… Fire under earth, light be blocked... That's it. Tethys is showing messy internal frequencies and unstable structures. Looks like after stuffing itself with all those frequencies, the Sovereign's completely scrambled inside.
If we can stir up the mess in its gut, even with something small, we might just throw him off balance. And then, maybe we can wrest the Earthrend Wedge back and readjust it to—
